questions.prompt: Why does OSPF compute shortest-cost path, not shortest-hop?

choices.text: Hops don't reflect link quality. A path through 5 fast (10Gbps) links is better than 2 slow (10Mbps) links — but shortest-hop routing would pick the slow path. OSPF uses link cost (typically inversely proportional to bandwidth: 10^8 / bandwidth) so faster links have lower cost, and Dijkstra picks the route with lowest total cost. Result: traffic flows over fast paths even if they're longer in hop count. Operators can manually tune costs to engineer specific behavior — make a backup link expensive so it's only used when the primary fails, or make a WAN link expensive to keep traffic local. This bandwidth-aware routing is fundamental to modern interior routing; hop-count-only is the old RIP approach (essentially obsolete). Same logic applies in BGP route selection at the MED level for some configurations.

questions.explanation: Hop count ignores link speed. Cost (inverse of bandwidth) picks fast links over many-hops-of-slow.

questions.prompt: Your Kubernetes cluster's pods can't reach pods on other nodes. CNI suspected. Tool to diagnose?

choices.text: Layered approach: (1) `kubectl get pods -o wide` — check pod IPs and which node each is on, (2) from a pod on node A, try to ping a pod IP on node B (use `kubectl exec`) — does L3 work? (3) `kubectl get networkpolicy` — is there a NetworkPolicy blocking the traffic? (4) check the CNI plugin's logs (Calico, Cilium, etc.) — most have pod-network-validation tools, (5) `tcpdump` on the node's host network and the pod's veth interface to see where packets disappear, (6) look at routing tables on each node — `ip route` shows the CIDRs assigned to each node and the routes between them, the CNI should populate these. Common causes: NetworkPolicy too restrictive, CNI plugin crashed, MTU mismatch (overlay headers add ~50 bytes, fragmentation drops), node-to-node routing broken (firewall on the underlying network). Systematic CNI debug; not 'restart and hope.'

questions.explanation: Pod IPs + ping + NetworkPolicy + CNI logs + tcpdump + routing tables. Systematic CNI diagnosis.
